features.summary: Tesla reported a breakthrough 2021 with 90% volume growth despite supply chain challenges, achieving over 14% GAAP operating margin and $5.5 billion net income. The company began production at Austin and Berlin facilities, with Austin producing Model Y vehicles featuring structural battery packs and 4680 cells. Tesla expects significant growth above 50% in 2022, though supply chain constraints, particularly chip shortages, remain the primary limiting factor. The company will not introduce new vehicle models in 2022 to focus on scaling existing production.
features.management_outlook: Management expects comfortable growth above 50% in 2022, achievable with just Fremont and Shanghai factories. Supply chain and logistics will continue to determine growth pace, with chip constraints expected to alleviate by end of 2022 or 2023. Tesla plans to announce new factory locations by end of 2022 and aims to have 80% of US customers able to access Tesla Insurance by year-end. The company prioritizes Full Self-Driving development, with Elon expressing confidence in achieving safer-than-human autonomy in 2022.
features.risks: Primary risks include ongoing supply chain constraints and chip shortages limiting production capacity. Inflation and rising commodity prices are pressuring costs, with uncertain near-term impact on gross margins. New factory ramps at Austin and Berlin will create inefficiencies and higher fixed costs in the short term. The company faces regulatory challenges with insurance expansion and uncertainty around achieving Full Self-Driving milestones despite management confidence.
features.key_drivers: Key growth drivers include ramping Austin and Berlin production, expanding Model Y mix which carries higher margins, and continued Shanghai localization benefits. The 4680 battery program is progressing with first deliveries expected in Q1 2022, though not constraining 2022 volumes. Full Self-Driving represents the most significant long-term value driver, potentially transforming vehicle economics through autonomous capabilities. Energy storage business expected to grow significantly once supply constraints ease, with transition to iron-based chemistry for stationary storage.
